title: build: drop support for ostree
body:
it is going to be removed from containers/image as well, so no longer
depend on it.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Are we OK with doing this without any kind of deprecation warning? I don't
know if this is something we can merge without a release or two of notice

On Wed, Oct 30, 2019, 09:07 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> LGTM
>

> Are we OK with doing this without any kind of deprecation warning? I don't know if this is something we can merge without a release or two of notice

the ostree backend is not usable from Podman.  The only user is the Atomic CLI, so the ostree support really leaked from containers/image without any benefit.

I don't see an issue, with this going in now.  It was really  a mistake that we exposed it, and not likely to work.
/lgtm
Are there plans to remove ostree pulling?  AFAICT it's still referenced in  podman-pull.1.
@rhatdan This is the master branch, and I think it's gone after this PR.
